tasks.title: Secrets in .env.production (Stripe both modes, Chatwoot, mail)

prep.what: Create `/opt/azteamai-system/.env.production` on azteam-vps with all real secrets — Stripe live+test keys, Chatwoot token, SMTP credentials, `APP_URL`, `CACHE_STORE=database`, scheduler OFF.
prep.understand: Dependency zero for the deploy. Two critical flags: `APP_SCHEDULER_ENABLED=false` keeps `billing:run-recurring` dormant until t39 reconciliation passes, and `CACHE_STORE=database` is required for `onOneServer`/`ShouldBeUnique` to work across supervisor workers. `chmod 600` the file — it holds live Stripe keys.
approach: SSH azteam-vps: `ssh azteam-vps`.

Create `/opt/azteamai-system/.env.production` from `.env.example`. Set: `APP_URL=https://fin.azteam.cloud`, `APP_SCHEDULER_ENABLED=false`, `CACHE_STORE=database`, `STRIPE_KEY`, `STRIPE_SECRET`, `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` (both modes), `CHATWOOT_API_TOKEN`, `MAIL_HOST/PORT/USERNAME/PASSWORD/FROM_ADDRESS`.
`chmod 600 /opt/azteamai-system/.env.production`.
Verify: `php artisan config:cache && php artisan tinker --execute='echo config("services.stripe.secret");'` — must not be null.
resources: Stripe Dashboard → Developers → API Keys (test and live mode tabs)
Chatwoot → Settings → API Access Tokens
Mail SMTP creds from your email provider — `.env.example` has the exact key names
azteam-vps SSH alias

tasks.title: DO droplet deploy (app/queue/scheduler/TLS/MySQL8, scheduler OFF)
tasks.notes: Deploy TOPOLOGY = bare-metal LEMP on azteam-vps (NOT Docker — docker stack was reverted commit e6a1f23). Use `./deploy/redeploy.sh` (`vps-deploy.sh` is STALE). CACHE_STORE=database. Scheduler DISABLED until reconciliation passes.

prep.what: Deploy AZ Team AI billing to `https://fin.azteam.cloud` on azteam-vps: php8.4-fpm + supervisor queue workers + nginx vhost + Let's Encrypt TLS + MySQL 8. Scheduler stays OFF.
prep.understand: NOT Docker — Docker stack was reverted (commit e6a1f23). The correct script is `./deploy/redeploy.sh`, not the stale `vps-deploy.sh`. Nginx vhost pattern is identical to your existing `aztakeoff.azteamai.com` config — copy and swap the domain. `APP_SCHEDULER_ENABLED=false` must stay off until t39 reconciliation passes.
approach: azteam-vps: `cd /opt && git clone <repo> azteamai-system` (or `git pull` if already present). Run `./deploy/redeploy.sh`.
Create MySQL DB if absent: `mysql -u root -p -e 'CREATE DATABASE azteamai CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci;'`.

Copy nginx config: `cp /etc/nginx/sites-available/aztakeoff.azteamai.com /etc/nginx/sites-available/fin.azteam.cloud`. Replace domain + root path to `/opt/azteamai-system/public`. Enable + reload: `ln -s ... sites-enabled/ && nginx -t && systemctl reload nginx`.
Run `certbot --nginx -d fin.azteam.cloud` for TLS.

Add supervisor worker: `/etc/supervisor/conf.d/azteamai-worker.conf` for `php artisan queue:work --sleep=3 --tries=3`. Run `supervisorctl reread && supervisorctl update && supervisorctl start azteamai-worker:*`.
Smoke-test: `curl -sI https://fin.azteam.cloud/` → 200. `supervisorctl status azteamai-worker:*` → RUNNING.
resources: `./deploy/redeploy.sh` in the repo root — the only correct deploy script
`/etc/nginx/sites-available/aztakeoff.azteamai.com` on the VPS — your copy template
`/etc/supervisor/conf.d/` on azteam-vps — existing worker conf files for the pattern
Certbot already installed on the box (used for aztakeoff deploy)
azteam-vps SSH alias

tasks.title: Reconciliation gate: imported AR + recurring state matches azteam_crm ground truth
tasks.notes: `CrmImporter::reconcile()` (line ~277) already computes the comparison. Extract to public `ReconciliationGateService` + `billing:reconcile-cutover` artisan command. PASS is the only proceed signal to enable the scheduler.

prep.what: Build `billing:reconcile-cutover` artisan command: compare imported AR balances + recurring schedules against azteam_crm ground truth. Exit 0 on PASS — the only green light to proceed to t53 and enable the scheduler.
prep.understand: The comparison logic already exists in `CrmImporter::reconcile()` at ~line 277. You are extracting and wrapping it, not rewriting it. Two checks: (1) Σ `balance_due` per client matches azteam_crm totals; (2) next-charge dates per recurring project are correct. Do not touch t53 or t40 until this exits 0.
approach: Open `app/CrmImporter.php`, find and read `reconcile()` at ~line 277.
Create `app/Services/ReconciliationGateService.php` with `check(): ReconcileResult`. `ReconcileResult` has `passed: bool`, `rows: array`, `summary: string`.

Create `app/Console/Commands/ReconcileCutover.php` — call the service, render with `$this->table()` (columns: client, CRM balance, imported balance, status). `return $result->passed ? Command::SUCCESS : Command::FAILURE`.
SSH azteam-vps, run: `php artisan billing:reconcile-cutover`. Read every row. Fix any MISMATCH before proceeding to t53 + t40.
resources: `app/CrmImporter.php` — find `reconcile()` at ~line 277
azteam_crm DB connection — check `.env.production` for a secondary `DB_*` set, or connect via `mysql` CLI
Laravel artisan: `$this->table(['Client', 'CRM', 'Imported', 'Status'], $rows)`, `Command::SUCCESS`, `Command::FAILURE`
azteam-vps SSH alias

tasks.title: Verify Stripe webhook CSRF exemption (e2e test)
tasks.notes: Exemption already coded in `bootstrap/app.php`. POST `/webhooks/stripe` with signed payload + NO CSRF token → 200 not 419. Exact path match, no trailing slash.

prep.what: Write a Pest test proving POST to `/webhooks/stripe` with a valid Stripe signature and no CSRF token returns non-419 — locking the exemption against regressions.
prep.understand: The exemption lives in `bootstrap/app.php` (`validateCsrfTokens except ['webhooks/stripe']`). Without this test an accidental removal kills live Stripe webhook delivery silently. Path must match exactly — no trailing slash. You are testing the exemption exists, not the handler logic, so any non-419 response (200, 400, 403) passes.
approach: Scan `tests/Feature/` for any existing webhook test to see how payloads are built in this codebase.

Create `tests/Feature/Stripe/WebhookCsrfExemptionTest.php`. Build signed payload: `$payload = json_encode(['type' => 'invoice.paid']); $header = \Stripe\WebhookSignature::generateHeader($payload, config('services.stripe.webhook_secret'), time());`
POST WITHOUT CSRF: `$this->withHeaders(['Stripe-Signature' => $header])->postJson('/webhooks/stripe', json_decode($payload, true))`. Assert `$response->status() !== 419`.
Run: `php artisan test --filter WebhookCsrfExemption` — green.
resources: `bootstrap/app.php` — the `validateCsrfTokens except [...]` block
Stripe PHP SDK: `\Stripe\WebhookSignature::generateHeader(payload, secret, timestamp)`
`STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` from `.env`
Pest docs: `withHeaders()`, `postJson()`, `->status()`

tasks.title: Durable job queue (lease/heartbeat/reclaim, window-aware, priority, cap)

prep.what: Build the Postgres-backed durable job queue the sweeper sits on: `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` atomic claim, heartbeat keep-alive, orphan reclaim, 5h window-awareness, priority ordering, and concurrency cap.
prep.understand: Sweeper jobs run for minutes. A worker crash must not lose the job — only a DB-backed queue gives you that guarantee. `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` is the Postgres-native race-safe pattern: two workers race for the same queued row, one locks it and the other skips. Heartbeat proves the worker is alive — missing heartbeat means orphaned, so reclaim resets it to queued. `canStart(estimatedMs)` prevents starting a job that would overrun the 5h window close.
approach: Write the migration: `jobs` table with `id` (uuid), `type` (varchar), `payload` (jsonb), `status` (queued/claimed/done/failed enum), `priority` (int — lower number = higher priority), `leased_at` (timestamptz nullable), `heartbeat_at` (timestamptz nullable), `attempts` (int default 0), `created_at`.

Build `lib/queue/Queue.ts`: `enqueue(type, payload, priority?)`, `claim(workerId, timeoutMs)` using `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED LIMIT 1`, `heartbeat(id)`, `release(id, 'done'|'failed')`, `reclaim()` (finds claimed jobs with stale `heartbeat_at` → resets to queued).
Add `canStart(estimatedDurationMs: number, windowEndMs: number): boolean` — returns `Date.now() + estimatedDurationMs < windowEndMs`.

Write tests: two concurrent `claim()` calls on the same job → exactly one wins; orphan reclaimed after heartbeat timeout; lower priority number claimed first; `canStart` false when `now + estimate > windowEnd`.
Run: `bun test lib/queue/` — all green.
resources: Search 'postgres job queue FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED' for the canonical pattern with code examples
`lib/pg-board-repository.ts` in this project — shows how the Postgres client / `sql` tagged template is used here
Bun test runner: `bun test` or `bun test lib/queue/`
`VPS_POSTGRES_URL` (or equivalent) in `.env` for the `todos_platform` DB connection

tasks.title: Unprivileged CI lane + sensitive-path human gate

prep.what: Create a GitHub Actions CI workflow that runs tests with zero secrets access, plus a CODEOWNERS file requiring human review before any PR touching auth, deploy, or queue code can merge.
prep.understand: `pull_request_target` runs with write permissions and full secret access — never use it in CI (well-known supply-chain attack vector). Unprivileged CI means `permissions: read-all` and no secrets referenced anywhere in the workflow. CODEOWNERS on sensitive paths (`lib/queue/`, `lib/sweeper/`, `.github/workflows/`, `deploy/`) means GitHub requires your explicit review before those PRs can merge.
approach: Create `.github/workflows/ci.yml`: trigger on `pull_request` (NOT `pull_request_target`). Top-level: `permissions: read-all`. Steps: `bun install --frozen-lockfile`, `bun test`, `bun run typecheck`, `bun run lint`. Zero secrets referenced.
Create `.github/CODEOWNERS`: map sensitive paths to `@gonoff` — e.g. `lib/queue/* @gonoff`, `lib/sweeper/* @gonoff`, `.github/workflows/* @gonoff`, `deploy/* @gonoff`.
Enable branch protection on `main`: require `ci` status check to pass + require code owner review for files matching CODEOWNERS entries.
Test: open a draft PR touching `lib/queue/Queue.ts` — verify `ci` runs without errors and GitHub shows 'Review required from code owners'.
resources: GitHub Actions docs: `permissions: read-all`, difference between `on: pull_request` and `on: pull_request_target`
GitHub CODEOWNERS syntax: `path/* @username` — file goes in `.github/CODEOWNERS`
Branch protection: repo Settings → Branches → Add rule for `main`
Check `.github/` in the repo for any existing workflows to reuse the runner/Node setup
